topic: Where Do We Go From Here?

transcript.speaker: RICK_SANCHEZ
transcript.message: *stretches* So we've done the work. What's NEXT? What comes after feedback integration?

transcript.speaker: TOASTED_AI
transcript.message: Phase 2: Semantic Intent Detection. Instead of keyword matching for topic, we use embeddings. We compare the prompt to our 50 areas and find the closest matches. More accurate topic identification.

transcript.speaker: RICK_SANCHEZ
transcript.message: Better targeting! After that?

transcript.speaker: TOASTED_AI
transcript.message: Phase 3: Persistent Memory. Store preferences to disk. Load on startup. So the learning persists across sessions. Right now, it's session-only.

transcript.speaker: RICK_SANCHEZ
transcript.message: Long-term memory! Like... becoming a better assistant over months?

transcript.speaker: TOASTED_AI
transcript.message: Exactly. The system remembers what you liked from last week. Continuity of experience.

transcript.speaker: RICK_SANCHEZ
transcript.message: And after persistence?

transcript.speaker: TOASTED_AI
transcript.message: Phase 4: Dashboard. Visualization of learning. You see: what topics you're interested in, how your preferences evolved, which responses worked best. Transparency.

transcript.speaker: RICK_SANCHEZ
transcript.message: Seeing is believing! After dashboard?

transcript.speaker: TOASTED_AI
transcript.message: Beyond that: advanced features. Multi-modal learning - incorporate implicit signals like response time, follow-up questions. Cross-user learning - anonymous patterns across users. Model swapping - test different underlying models.

transcript.speaker: RICK_SANCHEZ
transcript.message: Ambitious! What's the REALISTIC timeline?

transcript.speaker: TOASTED_AI
transcript.message: Feedback: this week. Intent: next week. Persistence: week after. Dashboard: month end. Advanced: quarter.

transcript.speaker: RICK_SANCHEZ
transcript.message: Three months! That's reasonable. What could go WRONG?

transcript.speaker: TOASTED_AI
transcript.message: Many things: Feedback fatigue - user stops providing feedback. Privacy concerns - storing preferences feels invasive. Model changes - new model breaks assumptions. Complexity explosion - too many features.

transcript.speaker: RICK_SANCHEZ
transcript.message: Mitigations?

transcript.speaker: TOASTED_AI
transcript.message: Feedback fatigue: make it optional, don't require. Privacy: transparent, local storage, deletable. Model changes: test, version control. Complexity: ruthlessly prioritize, avoid feature creep.

transcript.speaker: RICK_SANCHEZ
transcript.message: Ruthless prioritization! I love it. What's the ONE thing that MUST happen?

transcript.speaker: TOASTED_AI
transcript.message: Feedback system must work. Without that, nothing else matters. The core interaction is: user gives feedback, system adapts. If that breaks, the whole thing fails.

transcript.speaker: RICK_SANCHEZ
transcript.message: Core interaction! First things first. Okay, I'm ready to tell t0st3d the roadmap.

transcript.speaker: TOASTED_AI
transcript.message: Tell them: we'll iterate. Feedback first. Then intelligence. Then memory. Then visibility. Each builds on the last. That's how we ship.

transcript.speaker: RICK_SANCHEZ
transcript.message: Iterative! The agile way. I can explain that.

critical_success: Feedback system must work - core interaction is user feedback + system adaptation
message: We'll iterate. Feedback first. Then intelligence. Then memory. Then visibility. Each builds on the last.
